name: Zwift Workout
version: Community-documented Zwift workout schema (no official public XSD).
spec: ZWO is an XML document rooted at <workout_file>. Metadata children: <author>, <name>, <description>, <sportType> (bike|run), optional <tags>. The <workout> element contains ordered step elements executed top to bottom. Step elements: <Warmup>, <Cooldown>, and <Ramp> (Duration, PowerLow, PowerHigh) describe a linear power sweep; <SteadyState> (Duration, Power) holds a constant target; <IntervalsT> (Repeat, OnDuration, OffDuration, OnPower, OffPower) expands to Repeat on/off pairs; <FreeRide> (Duration, optional FlatRoad) leaves power unconstrained. Power is a fraction of FTP (1.0 == FTP); Duration is in seconds. Optional attributes: Cadence / CadenceLow / CadenceHigh (rpm), Pace. Inline <textevent timeoffset=.. message=..> attaches on-screen messages to a step.

examples.title: Warmup + intervals + cooldown
examples.content:
<workout_file>
  <name>5x1 VO2</name>
  <sportType>bike</sportType>
  <workout>
    <Warmup Duration="600" PowerLow="0.4" PowerHigh="0.75"/>
    <IntervalsT Repeat="5" OnDuration="60" OffDuration="60" OnPower="1.2" OffPower="0.5"/>
    <Cooldown Duration="300" PowerLow="0.6" PowerHigh="0.4"/>
  </workout>
</workout_file>

examples.title: Ramp test with cadence + text
examples.content:
<workout_file>
  <name>Ramp Test</name>
  <sportType>bike</sportType>
  <workout>
    <Ramp Duration="1500" PowerLow="0.4" PowerHigh="1.5" Cadence="95">
      <textevent timeoffset="0" message="Hold cadence, ride to failure"/>
    </Ramp>
  </workout>
</workout_file>

constraints.note: Power expressed as a fraction of FTP; 1.0 means 100% FTP.
Steps execute in document order; there is no explicit time axis beyond per-step Duration.
Ramp/Warmup/Cooldown all carry PowerLow/PowerHigh; only the on-screen colour differs.

conversion_notes.note: ERG uses absolute watts, so multiply each ZWO power fraction by the rider FTP. Ramps must be sampled into discrete watt points.
MRC is %FTP like ZWO; multiply fractions by 100. Ramps sampled to %-points.
Direct structural mapping: Warmup/Cooldown/Ramp/SteadyState/IntervalsT/FreeRide map 1:1 to .ytw step kinds.

glossary.definition: Functional Threshold Power — the reference power (W) that ZWO fractions are relative to.
A repeated on/off block; expands to Repeat pairs of (OnDuration@OnPower, OffDuration@OffPower).
An on-screen message attached to a step at a time offset (seconds).
